decision.reason: WeiLab page is a bioinformatics lab site and does not provide evidence for AI researcher Jason Wei. No active card or People JSON dependency.

raw.title: WeiLab
raw.text:
WeiLab
* ## Welcome to WeiLab
The main work of our Lab is to develop artificial intelligence algorithms and their applications in Bioinformatics.
* [Learn More](#)
* [Contact Us](https://wei-group.net/contact)
* ## WELCOME TO WeiLab
We are looking for talented and motivated undergraduate and graduate students. If you are interested in Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and Bioinformatics, please feel free to contact with me!
* [Learn More](research.html)
* [Contact Us](contact.html)
### Research interests
* ##### Peptide Drug discovery
Our lab aims to developing an automated computational pipeline for peptide drug design and discovery, which involves bioactive peptide generation, prediction, and functional analysis (such as peptide toxicity).
* * ##### Epigenomic data analysis
Our lab focuses on developing computational methods to accurately annotate epigenetic modifications in whole-genome scale, and study their functions in gene regulatory network.
* * ##### Developing machine learning algorithms
Our lab also focuses on the development of generic machine learning algorithms and applied them for analyzing biological sequence data and imaging data. We particularly interest in designing new representation learning architectures to automatically learn and extract the functional patterns underlying different data.
### News
* **[Apr-27-2025]**Congrats! Our work**"A self-conformation-aware pre-training framework for molecular property prediction with substructure interpretability"**has been accepted for publication on**Nature Communications**!
* **[Oct-22-2024]**Congrats!**Prof. Wei**was promoted as Co-Editor-in-Chief of the journal -**Current Bioinformatics(Impact Factor=2.4)**!
* **[Oct-14-2024]**Congrats!**Prof. Wei**was appointed as Associate Editor of the journal -**Journal of Translational Medicine(Impact Factor=6.1)**!
* **[Jul-31-2024]**Congrats!**Prof. Wei**was promoted as an Associate Editor of the journal -**Biology Methods & Protocols**!
* **[Jun-28-2024]**Congrats!**Prof. Wei**was promoted as an Assistant Specialty Chief Editor of the journal -**Frontiers in Genetics**!
* **[Mar-4-2024]**Congrats!**Prof. Wei**was appointed as an Editor of journal**Methods (JCR-1)**!
* **[Sep-12-2023]**Our work**"Retrosynthesis prediction with an interpretable deep-learning framework based on molecular assembly tasks"**has been accepted for publication on**Nature Communications**.
* **[Aug-24-2023]**Congrats!**Prof. Wei**recieve the support by the**National Science Fund for Excellent Young Scholars of China (国家优秀青年基金)!**
* **[Mar-10-2023]**Our work**"ExamPle: explainable deep learning framework for the prediction of plant small secreted peptides"**has been accepted for publication on Bioinformatics.
* **[Feb-14-2023] Prof. Wei**is now guest editting a special issue in journal**'Biology Methods and Protocols' (Oxford University Press)**, focusing on**'[Deep learning with applications to epigenetic data analysis](https://academic.oup.com/biomethods/pages/call-for-papers-deep-learning-with-applications-to-epigenetic-data-analysis)'**. The journal would have its first Impact Factor in this Spring.**Related submission is very welcome**.
* **[Jan-29-2023]**Our work**"Explainable deep hypergraph learning modeling the peptide secondary structure prediction"**has been accepted for publication on**Advanced Science**.
* **[Jan-18-2023]**Our work**"DeepBIO: An automated and interpretable deep-learning platform for high-throughput biological sequence prediction, functional annotation, and visualization analysis"**has been accepted for publication on**Nucleic Acids Research**.
* **[Nov-17-2022] Prof. Wei**was named**"Highly Cited Researcher"**in Computer Science by Clarivate Analytics 2021! 
* **[Oct-3-2022]**Our work**" iDNA-ABF: multi-scale deep biological language learning model for the interpretable prediction of DNA methylations"**has been accepted for publication on**Genome Biology**.
* **[Jun-24-2022] Prof. Wei**was appointed as the Editorial Board Member of**"Biology Methods and Protocols" (Oxford University Press).**
* **[May-18-2022]**Our work**" Predicting protein-peptide binding residues via interpretable deep learning"**has been accepted for publication on**Bioinformatics**.
* **[Apr-26-2022]**Our collaborative work**"Recent advances in the prediction of subcellular localization of proteins and related topics."**has been accepted for publication on**Frontiers in Bioinformatics**.
* **[Apr-20-2022]**Our work**"scIMC: a platform for benchmarking comparison and visualization analysis of scRNA-seq data imputation methods"**has been accepted for publication on**Nucleic Acids Research**.
* [Learn More](https://wei-group.net/news)
### Global Visitors
### Recruitment
I'm looking for talented and motivated undergraduate and graduate students. If you are interested in Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and Bioinformatics, please feel free to contact with me!
### Contact Us
Room 404,Joint SDU-NTU Centre for Artificial Intelligence R

decision.reason: a16z Big Ideas video is institution-level content and the captured text does not establish Marc Andreessen as the speaker or subject. No active card or People JSON dependency.

raw.title: AI in 2026: 3 Predictions For What’s To Come (a16z Big Ideas)
raw.text:
AI is reshaping how discovery, connection, and business advantage are created.

In part three of Big Ideas 2026, we explore three shifts defining where AI goes next when the stakes are real and the impact compounds. Oliver Hsu explains how advances in AI reasoning and robotics are moving science toward autonomous labs, accelerating discovery while making interpretability essential. Bryan Kim explores how consumer AI is evolving beyond productivity toward connection, identity, and helping people feel seen. David Haber breaks down why the most durable AI companies are those where AI reinforces the business model itself, driving revenue, outcomes, and compounding advantage.

Timecodes:
0:00  Big Ideas for 2026
0:28  Autonomous Labs and AI in Scientific Discovery
3:55  Market Dynamics and Early Adopters in Autonomous Science
5:08  Public-Private Partnerships Accelerating AI-Driven Science
6:21  AI in Consumer Applications: From Productivity to Connectivity
7:08  AI and Human Connection: Startups vs. Incumbents
7:47  AI as a Relationship Facilitator
8:39  Personalization and the Future of Consumer AI
9:31  AI Reinforcing Business Models
10:05  Case Study: AI in Plaintiff Law and Lending
11:26  Compounding Advantages and Proprietary Data
12:29  Smarter Outcomes and the Future of AI-Driven Platforms

Resources:
Follow Oliver on X: https://twitter.com/oyhsu
Follow Bryan Kim on X: https://twitter.com/kirbyman01 
Follow David Haber on X: https://twitter.com/dhaber

Part 3: https://a16z.com/newsletter/big-ideas-2026-part-3

Follow our host: https://twitter.com/eriktorenberg

# Computer Science \> Artificial Intelligence
**arXiv:2511.07943**(cs)
[Submitted on 11 Nov 2025 ([v1](https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.07943v1)), last revised 14 Nov 2025 (this version, v2)]
# Title:Thinker: Training LLMs in Hierarchical Thinking for Deep Search via Multi-Turn Interaction
Authors:[Jun Xu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Xu,+J),[Xinkai Du](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Du,+X),[Yu Ao](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Ao,+Y),[Peilong Zhao](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Zhao,+P),[Yang Li](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Li,+Y),[Ling Zhong](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Zhong,+L),[Lin Yuan](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Yuan,+L),[Zhongpu Bo](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Bo,+Z),[Xiaorui Wang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Wang,+X),[Mengshu Sun](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Sun,+M),[Zhengke Gui](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Gui,+Z),[Dalong Zhang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Zhang,+D),[Zhaoyang Wang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Wang,+Z),[Qiwei Wang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Wang,+Q),[Yangyang Hou](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Hou,+Y),[Zhiying Yin](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Yin,+Z),[Haofen Wang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Wang,+H),[Huajun Chen](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Chen,+H),[Lei Liang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Liang,+L),[Jun Zhou](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Zhou,+J)
View a PDF of the paper titled Thinker: Training LLMs in Hierarchical Thinking for Deep Search via Multi-Turn Interaction, by Jun Xu and 19 other authors
[View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2511.07943)[HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2511.07943v2)> > Abstract:
> Efficient retrieval of external knowledge bases and web pages is crucial for enhancing the reasoning abilities of LLMs. Previous works on training LLMs to leverage external retrievers for solving complex problems have predominantly employed end-to-end reinforcement learning. However, these approaches neglect supervision over the reasoning process, making it difficult to guarantee logical coherence and rigor. To address these limitations, we propose Thinker, a hierarchical thinking model for deep search through multi-turn interaction, making the reasoning process supervisable and verifiable. It decomposes complex problems into independently solvable sub-problems, each dually represented in both natural language and an equivalent logical function to support knowledge base and web searches. Concurrently, dependencies between sub-problems are passed as parameters via these logical functions, enhancing the logical coherence of the problem-solving process. To avoid unnecessary external searches, we perform knowledge boundary determination to check if a sub-problem is within the LLM's intrinsic knowledge, allowing it to answer directly. Experimental results indicate that with as few as several hundred training samples, the performance of Thinker is competitive with established baselines. Furthermore, when scaled to the full training set, Thinker significantly outperforms these methods across various datasets and model sizes. The source code is available at [> this https URL
](https://github.com/OpenSPG/KAG-Thinker)> . Comments:|Accepted to AAAI 2026. Extended version with full Appendix|
Subjects:|Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Com

分类专栏： [大模型](https://blog.csdn.net/weixin_45838549/category_12607173.html) 文章标签： [学习](https://so.csdn.net/so/search/s.do?q=%E5%AD%A6%E4%B9%A0&t=all&o=vip&s=&l=&f=&viparticle=&from_tracking_code=tag_word&from_code=app_blog_art) [笔记](https://so.csdn.net/so/search/s.do?q=%E7%AC%94%E8%AE%B0&t=all&o=vip&s=&l=&f=&viparticle=&from_tracking_code=tag_word&from_code=app_blog_art) [人工智能](https://so.csdn.net/so/search/s.do?q=%E4%BA%BA%E5%B7%A5%E6%99%BA%E8%83%BD&t=all&o=vip&s=&l=&f=&viparticle=&from_tracking_code=tag_word&from_code=app_blog_art)

版权声明：本文为博主原创文章，遵循 [CC 4.0 BY-SA](http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/) 版权协议，转载请附上原文出处链接和本声明。

本文链接： [https://blog.csdn.net/weixin\_45838549/article/details/136748893](https://blog.csdn.net/weixin_45838549/article/details/136748893)

[大模型专栏收录该内容](https://blog.csdn.net/weixin_45838549/category_12607173.html)

8 篇文章

订阅专栏

原博链接： [https://lilianweng.github.io/posts/2023-06-23-agent/](https://lilianweng.github.io/posts/2023-06-23-agent/)

1. Agent关键组件

(1) 规划(Planning)

- 任务分解
 - 思维链 [**Chain of thought**](https://lilianweng.github.io/posts/2023-03-15-prompt-engineering/#chain-of-thought-cot) (CoT; [Wei et al. 2022](https://arxiv.org/abs/2201.11903)) ：逐步思考能力
 - 思维树Tree of Thoughts ( [Yao et al. 2023](https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.10601))：广度优先搜索(BFS)、深度优先搜索(DFS)
- 自我反思：纠正以前错误，迭代改进
 - ReAct ( [Yao et al. 2023](https://arxiv.org/abs/2210.03629))将动作空间扩展为特定于任务的离散动作和语言空间的组合
 - Reflexion ( [Shinn & Labash 2023](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366))是一个为Agent提供动态记忆和自我反思能力以提高推理能力的框架
 - Chain of Hindsight (CoH; [Liu et al. 2023](https://arxiv.org/abs/2302.02676))鼓励模型通过显式地呈现一系列过去的输出来改进自己的输出，每个输出都有反馈注释

(2) 记忆(Memory)

- 人脑中的记忆类型：感觉记忆、短期记忆、长期记忆


> 外部记忆可以缓解注意力持续时间有限的限制。标准做法是将信息的嵌入表示保存到支持快速最大内积搜索(MIPS)的矢量存储数据库中。为了优化检索速度，通常的选择是近似最近邻(ANN)算法。


(3) 工具使用(Tool use)

- MRKL ( [Karpas et al. 2022](https://arxiv.org/abs/2205.00445))
- TALM (Tool Augmented Language Models; [Parisi et al. 2022](https://arxiv.org/abs/2205.12255))
- Toolformer ( [Schick et al. 2023](https://arxiv.org/abs/2302.04761))
- HuggingGPT ( [Shen et al. 2023](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.17580))：1) 任务规划 2)模型选择 3)任务执行 4)响应生成
- API-Bank ( [Li et al. 2023](https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.08244))

1. 挑战

- 有限的上下文长度
- 长期计划和任务分解中的挑战
- 自然语言接口的可靠性

06-306496

[Planning 规划子目标和分解：代理将大型任务分解为较小的、可管理的子目标，从而能够高效处理复杂任务。反思和细化：智能体可以对过去的行为进行自我批评和自我反省，从错误中吸取教训，并为未来的步骤进行提炼，从而提高最终结果的质量。Memory 记忆短期记忆：我认为所有的上下文 _学习_（参见提示工程）都是利用模型的短期记忆来 _学习_ 的。长期记忆：这为代理提供了长时间保留和调用（无限）信息的能力，通常是通过利用外部载体存储和快速检索。Tool use 工具使用。](https://dreamit.blog.csdn.net/article/details/131467998)

[OpenAI 的 AI 安全负责人： _LLM_ 支持的自主代理 _LLM_ _Powered_ _Autonomous_ _Agents_](https://dreamit.blog.csdn.net/article/details/132059497)

[AI天才研究院](https://blog.csdn.net/universsky2015)

08-022183

[AI助手可以将用户输入解析为多个任务：\[{“task”：task，“id”，task\_id，“dep”：dependency\_task\_ids，“args”：{“text”：text，“image”：URL，“audio “：网址，“视频”：网址}}\]。一个有趣的观察结果是，虽然基于 _LLM_ 的评估得出的结论是 GPT _-_ 4 和 ChemCrow 的表现几乎相当，但专家针对解决方案的完成度和化学正确性进行的人工评估表明，ChemCrow 的性能大幅优于 GPT _-_ 4。您必须首先以直截了当的方式回答用户的请求。](https://dreamit.blog.csdn.net/article/details/132059497)

[(读书 _笔记_ _-_ 大模型) _LLM_ _Powered_ _Autonomous_ _Agents_](https://blog.csdn.net/junerli/article/details/138510030)

[junerli的博客](https://blog.csdn.net/junerli)

05-061965

[有限的上下文长度： _LLM_ 处理上下文信息的能力有限，尽管 self _-_ reflection 等机制可以从过去的错误中 _学习_，但更长或无限的上下文窗口将会带来很大的好处。感知记忆：这是记忆的早期阶段，它能够在原始刺激结束后保持对感官信息（视觉、听觉等）的印象。显式、陈述性记忆：这是对事实和事件的记忆，指的是那些可以有意识地回忆起来的记忆，包括情景记忆（事件和经过）和语义记忆（事实和概念）；反思和完善：智能体可以对过去的行为展开自我批评和自我反思，从错误中吸取教训，并针对未来的步骤进行完善，提高最终结果的质量。](https://blog.csdn.net/junerli/article/details/138510030)

[人类反馈数据是一组 Dh={(x,yi,ri,zi)}i=1n ，其中 x 是提示，每个 yi 是模型完成， ri 是对 yi 的人类评分， zi 是相应的人类提供的事后诸葛亮反馈。Reflexion 具有标准的强化 _学习_ 设置，其中奖励模型提供简单的二元奖励，动作空间遵循 ReAct 中的设置，特定任务的动作空间通过语言增强以实现复杂的推理步骤。您必须首先以直接的方式

decision.reason: OpenReview capture is an anonymous multimodal Theory-of-Mind paper and contains no Tom Brown evidence. No active card or People JSON dependency.

raw.text:
Under review as a conference paper at ICLR 2025
SCALING MULTIMODAL THEORY-OF-MIND WITH
WEAK-TO-STRONG BAYESIAN REASONING
Anonymous authors
Paper under double-blind review
ABSTRACT
1 Theory-of-Mind (ToM) enables individuals to the mental states of the others, such
2 as thoughts, beliefs, and desires. To replicate this cognitive ability in machines,
3 especially under complex multimodal environments, recent advances combine
4 Bayesian-based state inference with deep learning models to estimate mental states,
5 where the Bayesian model handles state transitions and a language model (LM)
6 estimates the likelihood of intermediate states. However, while post-training an LM
7 to specialise in ToM tasks improves performance, the computational cost increases
8 as the LM scales, limiting the model size to 7 billion parameters. Despite this post9 training process, smaller LMs still struggle with the physical and mental modelling
10 demands of ToM due to their limited world knowledge and reasoning capacity. To
11 address this, we propose a scalable solution that leverages the strengths of larger
12 LMs (up to 70 and 405 billion parameters, respectively), including their vast world
13 knowledge and atomic-level reasoning capabilities, without increasing post-training
14 resource requirements. Our method transfers ToM-specific behaviours from a post15 trained small LM to guide the latent reasoning of a larger LM during test time. This
16 weak-to-strong control mechanism enables the larger LM to improve Bayesian
17 likelihood estimation at each inference step, harnessing its reasoning power in
18 ToM scenarios while reducing the need for additional training resources. Extensive
19 experiments demonstrate the significant effectiveness of our scaled approach. It is
20 better at inferring human mental states in complex and interactive environments,
21 outperforming the state-of-the-art solution by ∼ 4.6% across multiple tasks on the
22 multimodal ToM benchmark and unseen scenarios.
23 1 INTRODUCTION
24 A key aspect of human social cognition is Theory-of-Mind (ToM)—the ability to comprehend and
25 attribute mental states such as beliefs, desires, and intentions to ourselves and others. This capacity
26 allows individuals to recognize that others may have perspectives and motivations distinct from
27 their own, forming the foundation of social understanding and interaction (Dennett, 1988; Gopnik &
28 Wellman, 2012). Building on this concept, a critical challenge lies in enabling artificial intelligence
29 (AI) to acquire human-level ToM capabilities. Equipping AI systems with such abilities could
30 significantly enhance their potential for human-like interactions and unlock broader capacities for
31 commonsense and context-aware reasoning (Lake et al., 2017; Wu et al., 2021; Ma et al., 2023).
32 Among the ongoing efforts to advance ToM abilities in machines, two broadly classed strategies are
33 the highly structured Bayesian approaches and the less structured end-to-end approaches, respectively:
34 (i) Bayesian ToM models use cognitively structured probabilistic frameworks to represent causal
35 relationships between the mind and the world, enabling inverse inference of mental states from sparse
36 behavioral observations (Baker et al., 2017; Jara-Ettinger, 2019; Shu et al., 2021). These models are
37 highly interpretable and support key ToM functions like explanation and inductive learning, excelling
38 in well-defined domains with precise predictions and few-shot generalization (Shum et al., 2019;
39 Zhi-Xuan et al., 2022). However, they require extensive inductive constraints (e.g., abstractions,
40 priors, and causal relationships) provided by experts, limiting their scalability to complex and
41 unconstrained practical environments. (ii) End-to-end models of ToM involve training deep models
42 directly on ToM data (Rabinowitz et al., 2018; Shu et al., 2021; Sclar et al., 2022). These models,
43 such as ToMnet (Rabinowitz et al., 2018), autonomously learn complex patterns and relationships
44 from data without explicitly concepts like agents, beliefs, and goals. However, because these models
1
Under review as a conference paper at ICLR 2025
45 do not explicitly encode principles of physics or theories of psychology, they lack transparency and
46 may draw conclusions that violate physical laws, logical coherence, or commonsense reasoning.
47 Additionally, their data-driven nature makes them less reliable in, and adaptable to, dynamic or
48 novel environments, particularly where data is sparse or unrepresentative (Sap et al., 2022; Zhi-Xuan
49 et al., 2022; Ullman, 2023; Strachan et al., 2024).
50 The recent interdisciplinary milestone, BIPALM (Bayesian inverse planning accelerated by language
51 model), merges the strengths of Bayesian ToM models and deep learning to facilitate robust reasoning
52 in complex multimodal scenarios (Jin et al., 2024). It uses a Bayesian ToM model to predict agents’
53 mental states in mul

raw.title: Tom Brown Park
raw.text:
![](https://www.talgov.com/Uploads//Uploads/Public/Images/Banners/header2.jpg)

![](https://www.talgov.com/Uploads//Uploads/Public/Images/Banners/header2.jpg)

| |
| --- |
| ### Tom Brown Park![Tom Brown Park Playground](https://www.talgov.com/uploads/public/images/parks/parks/tombrown.jpg) **Hours of Operation**: Sunrise to Sunset Located in the eastern part of Tallahassee, just east of Capital Circle SE between Mahan Drive and Apalachee Parkway, sits a huge park with everything you could want to do in it. This is the City of Tallahassee's premier place to play, with numerous baseball and softball fields, tennis courts, and wide open spaces Tom Brown Park also hosts many of Tallahassee's special events, including the 4th of July Fireworks Show. Large special events are held here periodically, so you will be sure to frequent this park quite a lot. The park was dedicated on January 30, 1977 and named after Thomas Irvin Brown, Jr., a coach for Leon High School (1944-1946). He was also a coach for the Babe Ruth League for 20 years and a Leon County Commissioner for 14 years. His time, efforts and energies touched the lives of many of our leading young citizens. Tom Brown played the game of sports by giving it his all! - [Tom Brown Park Map](https://www.talgov.com/Uploads/Public/Documents/parks/parks/tombrownpermit.pdf) (PDF) - [Disc Golf](https://www.talgov.com/parks/sports-discgolf.aspx) - [Take a glance at the Magnolia Mountain Bike Trail](https://www.talgov.com/Uploads/Public/Documents/parks/parks/magnoliatrail.pdf) (PDF) - [See the Lafayette Passage Paddling Trail](https://www.talgov.com/parks/parks-lafayette.aspx) - [Check out the trails available at City parks](https://www.talgov.com/Uploads/Public/Documents/parks/parks/trails.pdf)(PDF) |

the Simons Foundation and member institutions.](https://confluence.cornell.edu/x/ALlRF)
# [![arxiv logo](https://static.arxiv.org/images/arxiv-logo-one-color-white.svg)](https://arxiv.org/)
